short_summary: Iran deal timing disputed; Tren de Aragua strike
rationale: US-Iran deal remains the dominant story with conflicting signals on timing; Tren de Aragua leader killing is a continuation of established airstrike policy with modest domestic resonance.

top_events.title: Trump says US-Iran deal to be signed Sunday; Tehran casts doubt
top_events.summary: Conflicting signals on deal timing create market and policy uncertainty for Americans — a signed deal could lower gas prices and reduce Middle East military exposure, but no resolution yet.

top_events.title: Israel attacks Beirut on same day Iran deal reportedly near signing
top_events.summary: Israeli strikes on Beirut complicate the US-Iran deal timeline and risk drawing the US deeper into Middle East tensions, affecting both military posture and oil markets.

top_events.title: US kills leader of Venezuela's Tren de Aragua gang in air strike
top_events.summary: Trump administration claims a successful strike against a high-profile gang leader — continuation of established policy, modest domestic political impact with no major new escalation.
